未来集团（Future Group）正在寻求临时禁令，以禁止其合作伙伴亚马逊向监管机构和其他当局致函，以引起对这两家印度巨人之间的交易的担忧，并终止了这笔交易。
 周一，德里高等法院的裁决是合作伙伴亚马逊与未来集团之间高额争夺战的最新一集。去年，亚马逊以超过1亿美元的价格收购了Future一家未上市公司中的49％的股份。亚马逊在法庭文件中表示，作为该交易的一部分，未来不能将资产出售给竞争对手。
  Future Group首席执行官兼创始人Kishore Biyani在最近的一次虚拟会议上说，在冠状病毒大流行使印度现金短缺之后，今年情况发生了变化。八月份，Future Group表示已与印度最大的零售连锁店Ambani的信实工业（Reliance Industries）达成协议，以34亿美元的价格出售其零售，批发，物流和仓储业务。
 几个月后，亚马逊通过与新加坡的一名仲裁员进行了抗议，要求法院阻止印度零售巨头之间的交易。亚马逊于10月下旬从新加坡仲裁法院获得紧急救济，这暂时阻止了Future Group继续进行出售。
 但目前尚不清楚该裁决是否会在印度法院面前解决任何问题-直到今天。如此之多，以至于新加坡仲裁法院宣布裁决之日，Future Group and Reliance在一份声明中表示，将继续进行这项交易。
 亚马逊还联系了印度监管机构印度竞争委员会，以阻止交易。但是，印度竞争委员会批准了印度公司之间的交易。在较早的听证会上，Future Group的律师将亚马逊为阻止Future Group的交易而比喻为东印度公司，该公司是英国贸易公司，其抵达印度开始了近200年的殖民统治。
 咨询公司BCG和印度当地贸易组织印度零售商协会称，到2025年，印度零售市场的规模将激增至1.3万亿美元，高于去年的7,000亿美元。在线购物约占印度所有零售的3％。
note_en:
Future Group was seeking an ad-interim injunction to restrain its partner Amazon from writing to regulators and other authorities to raise concerns over — and halt — the deal between the two Indian giants.
 The Delhi High Court ruling on Monday is the latest episode in the high-stake battle between partners Amazon and Future Group. Amazon  bought 49% in one of Future’s unlisted firms last year in a deal that was valued at over $100 million. As part of that deal, Future could not have sold assets to rivals, Amazon said in court filings.
  Things changed this year after the coronavirus pandemic starved the Indian firm of cash, Future Group chief executive and founder Kishore Biyani said at a recent virtual conference. In August, Future Group said that it had  reached an agreement with Ambani’s Reliance Industries, which runs India’s largest retail chain, to sell its retail, wholesale, logistics, and warehousing businesses for $3.4 billion.
 Months later, Amazon protested the deal by reaching an arbitrator in Singapore and asked the court to block the deal between the Indian retail giants. Amazon  secured emergency relief from the arbitration court in Singapore in late October that temporarily halted Future Group from going ahead with the sale.
 But it remained unclear whether that ruling would hold any water in front of Indian courts — until today. So much so that on the day Singapore arbitration court announced its ruling, Future Group and Reliance said in a statement that will be going ahead with the deal.
 Amazon had also reached out to the Competition Commission of India, the Indian watchdog, to block the deal. Competition Commission of India, however,  approved the deal between the Indian firms. In earlier hearings, lawyers for Future Group likened Amazon’s effort to block Future Group’s deal to the East India Company, the British trading house whose arrival in India kicked off nearly 200 years of colonial rule.
 At stake is India’s retail market that is estimated to balloon to $1.3 trillion by 2025, up from $700 billion last year, according to consultancy firm BCG and local trade group Retailers’ Association India. Online shopping accounts for about 3% of all retail in India.
title: 印度法院驳回零售业巨头未来集团对亚马逊的请求
title_en: Indian court rejects retail giant Future Group’s plea against Amazon

note:
软银投资顾问公司管理着两个愿景基金，并且可能会继续向SPAC靠拢，据报道还有两个在运作中。
  软银投资顾问公司首席执行官拉杰夫·米斯拉（Rajeev Misra）于10月在米尔肯研究所全球会议上发表讲话时表示，他正在计划建立SPAC。 SPAC将为愿景基金提供另一种投资私营公司的方式，并允许公众投资软银的投资组合。
 SPAC是为合并或收购其他公司而创建的空白支票公司，并且作为传统股票市场首次亮相的替代产品，今年已经获得了普及。
 这将是软银的第一个SPAC，但其投资组合公司之一，房地产平台OpenDoor，最近通过SPAC上市。其另一项投资是印尼电子商务巨头Tokopedia，在因大流行而制定了IPO计划后，也正在考虑通过由Richard Li和Peter Thiel支持的SPAC进行公开发行。
note_en:
SoftBank Investment Advisers manages the two Vision Funds and may continue leaning into SPACs, with two more reportedly in the works.
  The conglomerate first revealed its SPAC plans in October when SoftBank Investment Advisers chief executive officer Rajeev Misra  said he was planning a SPAC while speaking at the Milken Institute Global Conference. An SPAC would give the Vision Fund another way of investing in private companies, and also allow the public to invest in SoftBank’s portfolio picks.
 SPACs are blank-check companies created for the purpose of merging or acquiring other companies, and have gained popularity this year as an alternative to traditional stock market debuts.
 While this would be SoftBank’s first SPAC, one of its portfolio companies, real-estate platform OpenDoor, recently went public through an SPAC. Another one of its investments, Indonesian e-commerce giant Tokopedia, is  also considering going public through a SPAC backed by Richard Li and Peter Thiel, after putting its IPO plans because of the pandemic.
title: 据报道，软银将在周一申请SPAC
title_en: SoftBank will reportedly file for a SPAC on Monday

note:
该公司扬言要起诉该杂志后，已经撤回了一篇描述一名年轻女子试图通过食用Herbalife®产品来减轻体重的死亡的论文。
    康宝莱营养公司开发和销售用于减肥的植物性膳食补充剂。根据Wikipedia的说法，他们的奶昔，蛋白质棒，茶和其他产品的58％是在美国和中国的公司拥有的制造厂生产的，其余的则由第三方承包商制造。
 2016年，联邦贸易委员会（FTC）投诉该公司被视为“金字塔计划”。投诉获得了2亿美元的和解。
 此外，该公司因其关于健康益处的声明的准确性以及其某些产品中存在铅和其他有毒成分而受到批评。 Herbalife Wikipedia页面列出了世界各地的医院，这些医院报告了使用Herbalife产品的患者肝脏受损的情况。
   该文件（现在已被该杂志删除）描述了一名印度妇女的案例报告，该妇女的BMI为32.1（肥胖），他们食用了康宝莱减肥产品。根据现已撤消的文件，印度是康宝莱产品增长最快的市场。
 该名女子在一家营养俱乐部购买了产品，该俱乐部后来被喀拉拉邦政府关闭。使用该产品2个月后，她出现了黄疸病，并被送进医院，被诊断出肝坏死。
   由于作者无法从家庭中获得其余的减肥产品用于毒理学分析，因此他们从印度喀拉拉邦的同一位卖家和在线商店购买了类似的康宝莱产品。总共对八种产品进行了气相色谱，质谱和其他方法进行化学和微生物分析。 
化学分析检测到所有八种产品中的重金属，包括镉，汞和铅，以及其他有毒化学物质。此外，16S rRNA基因测序发现大多数产品中存在一系列细菌污染物。
 作者描述了其他使用康宝莱的具有肝毒性的受试者的类似病例报告，并提出了这些不受管制的康宝莱产品的重金属和细菌污染的可能性（可能与未知的有毒植物化学成分结合使用）可能导致了这些发病率。
  根据第一作者Cyriac Abby Philips（@drabbyphilips）的Twitter帖子（存档），几位与康宝莱公司或相关公司合作的教授随后因缺乏证据而要求该期刊撤回论文。
  编辑要求作者对这些问题写出科学的反驳。康宝莱的担忧和反驳都发表在了该期刊上，但随后被删除[PDF副本]。
 接下来，一家律师事务所威胁要采取法律行动，该杂志决定在没有明确科学理由的情况下撤回该论文。
   在违反COPE准则的情况下，尽管为文章分配了编号（Clin Exp Hepatol.Mar-Apr 2019; 9（2）：268-272），但该原始论文已被期刊完全删除。
 COPE指南指出，原始纸张应保持可用状态，但应清楚地标记为缩回。相反，发行人Elsevier删除了该论文，并用简短的撤回通知将其替换： 
“该文章发表于2019年3月至4月的《临床和实验性肝病杂志》（“ JCEH”）上，应JCEH主编和印度国家研究协会的要求而删除。肝脏（INASL）。 INASL和JCEH不再支持本文的内容和结论，因为科学的方法论，对文章基础数据的分析和解释不足以得出结论，因此删除该文章后，就不再依赖该文章。 “
  这种情况特别具有讽刺意味，因为我对许多其他Elsevier论文提出了科学问题，这些论文有严重的图像问题或有缺陷的非随机羟基氯喹结果。这些问题都没有得到解决，论文也没有被触及。
 但是，一旦有实力的公司因为对论文不满意而威胁要起诉出版商，事情就会突然收回-这是没有科学根据的。
  我希望作者能找到一个更有勇气的出版社来重新发表论文。
note_en:
A paper describing the death of a young woman trying to lose weight by consuming Herbalife® products has been withdrawn after the company threatened to sue the journal.
    Herbalife Nutrition develops and sells plant-based dietary supplements for weight loss. According to  Wikipedia, 58 percent of their shakes, protein bars, teas, and other products are made in company-owned manufacturing facilities in the US and China, with the remainder manufactured by third-party contractors.
 In 2016 the FTC filed a  complaint that the company was being run as a “pyramid scheme.” The complaint was  settled for $200 million.
 In addition the company has been criticized for the accuracy of their claims about health benefits, and also for the presence of lead and other toxic ingredients in several of their products. The Herbalife Wikipedia page lists hospitals all over the world that have reported  liver damage in patients using Herbalife products.
   The paper — now removed by the journal — describes a case report of a young woman in India with a BMI of 32.1 (obese) who consumed Herbalife slimming products. According to the now-removed paper, India is the fastest growing market for Herbalife products.
 The woman bought the products at a nutritional club that was later shut down by the Government of Kerala. After 2 months of using the products, she developed jaundice, and was admitted to the hospital where she was diagnosed with liver necrosis.
   Since the authors were unable to obtain the remainder of the slimming products from the family for a toxicology analysis, they bought similar Herbalife products from the same seller in Kerala, India, and from online stores. In total, eight products were subjected to gas chromatography, mass spectroscopy, and other methods for chemical and microbial analysis.
 The chemical analysis detected heavy metals, including cadmium, mercury, and lead, as well as other toxic chemicals in all eight products. In addition, 16S rRNA gene sequencing revealed a range of bacterial contaminants in most of the products.
 The authors describe similar case reports of other Herbalife-using subjects with hepatotoxicity, and raise the possibility that the heavy metals and bacterial contamination of these unregulated Herbalife products — maybe in combination with unknown toxic phytochemical constituents — could have contributed to these morbidities.
  As per a  Twitter thread [ archived] by first author Cyriac Abby Philips ( @drabbyphilips), several professors working with/at Herbalife or associated companies then requested that the journal should retract the paper because of a lack of evidence.
  The authors were asked by the editors to write a scientific rebuttal to the concerns. Both the Herbalife concerns and the rebuttal were published in the journal — but subsequently removed [ PDF copy].
 Next, a law firm threatened legal action, upon which the journal decided to retract the paper with no clear scientific reason.
   In a breach of COPE guidelines, and despite the fact that the article had been assigned an issue number ( Clin Exp Hepatol. Mar-Apr 2019;9(2):268-272), the original paper was completely removed by the journal.
 COPE guidelines state that the original paper should remain available, but be clearly marked as retracted. Instead, publisher Elsevier removed the paper, and replaced it with a short retraction notice:
 “ This article, which was published in the March-April 2019 issue of the Journal of Clinical and Experimental Hepatology (“JCEH”), has been removed at the request of the JCEH’s Editor-in-Chief and the Indian National Association for the Study of the Liver (INASL). INASL and JCEH no longer support the content of and conclusions drawn in the article because the scientific methodology, analysis and interpretation of data underlying the article were insufficient for the conclusions drawn, and, with its removal, the article can no longer be relied upon.“
  This case is particularly ironic because I have raised scientific concerns about dozens of other Elsevier papers with severe image problems or flawed non-randomized hydroxychloroquine results. None of these concerns have been addressed, and the papers have not been touched.
 But as soon as powerful companies threaten to sue a publisher because they are unhappy about a paper, suddenly things get retracted — with no scientific reason.
  I hope the authors can find a publishing house with more courage that will republish their paper.
title: 有关法律威胁后删除与康宝莱相关的患者死亡的论文
title_en: Paper about Herbalife-related patient death removed after legal threats
